excerpt: Safety We continue to advise exercise a high degree of caution for the cities of Guadalajara and Puerto Vallarta and we advise exercise a high degree of caution for the other areas in the state of Jalisco. Mexico has a high risk of violent crime, including murder, armed robbery, sexual assault, and kidnapping. Other travel options and routes may have higher security risks. Be alert to the potential risks around drink spiking and methanol poisoning through consuming alcoholic drinks. Only use ATMs in secure areas, such as hotels, banks and shopping centres during daytime.

Secure .gov websites use HTTPS lock ( ) or https:// means you’ve safely connected to the .gov website. Share sensitive information only on official, secure websites. Citizens.” More information Worldwide Caution: The Department of State advises Americans worldwide to exercise increased caution. They include homicide, kidnapping, carjacking, sexual assault, and robbery. There is a risk of terrorist violence, including terrorist attacks and other activity in Mexico. government employees may not travel to certain high-risk areas, which may be within states that include low-risk areas. Risks in specific areas Expand all Level: 4 - Do not travel State of Colima Do not travel due to terrorism , crime , and kidnapping. There is a risk of violence in the state from terrorist groups, cartels, gangs and criminal organizations. Shooting incidents between criminal groups have injured or killed bystanders.

excerpt: Safety and security - Mexico travel advice - GOV.UK Cookies on GOV.UK We use some essential cookies to make this website work. Warning FCDO advises against all but essential travel to parts of Mexico. Subscriptions Get email alerts Safety and security This guide also has safety advice for regions of Mexico . Find out how to reduce your risk from terrorism while abroad . Demonstrations can be tense, confrontational and potentially violent. Crime Crime is a risk to foreign nationals in Mexico, particularly in major cities and tourist resort areas. You should: research your destination thoroughly only travel during daylight hours when possible monitor local media tell trusted contacts your travel plans If you want to report a crime, do so immediately at the nearest branch of the state prosecutor’s office (‘Agencia del Ministerio úblico’). The Mexico City Command and Control Centre (‘Centro de Atención a Emergencias y Protección Ciudadana de la Ciudad de éxico’) has information and advice on safety in Mexico City in Spanish. Protecting your belongings Pickpocketing and theft are common, including on public transport.
